_comment: Frozen reproducible-node environment lock for ApprenticeOps waves. run.py --preflight reads this and REFUSES to start unless the live node matches (or you pass --allow-unlocked). This exists because wave1 (results.var, Turbo OFF, RAPL package-0) and wave2 (results.wave2, Turbo ON, RAPL psys/package-0 mixed) drifted: wave2 was launched without scripts/node-power.sh setup, so the prior run's teardown trap had restored Turbo, and RAPL_DOMAIN was unpinned. These env facts were never recorded in the data, so the drift was invisible. This manifest + the env.* provenance fields run.py now stamps make a wave's power/energy regime explicit and enforceable.
name: apprenticeops-wave-env-v1
frozen_from: results.var.jsonl (wave1), 2026-06-17..18, launched via scripts/run-experiment.sh + scripts/node-power.sh setup

cpu._comment: Set by scripts/node-power.sh setup. no_turbo=1 disables Turbo Boost; min=max=100 perf_pct pins to the non-turbo BASE clock (i5-8350U = 1.70 GHz). wave1 observed freq ceiling 1708 MHz; wave2 (turbo on) reached 3602 MHz.

energy._comment: RAPL domain MUST be pinned. package-0 = CPU package only (what wave1 used, 100%). psys = whole-SoC (reads higher; 760 wave2 rows used it -> not comparable). Pin via RAPL_DOMAIN=package-0.

telemetry._comment: perf counters must be readable for membw.series + perf.core. wave2 silently lost these on ~14% of runs (perf_event_paranoid too high or perf unavailable). Require paranoid<=2.

model_runtime._comment: Ollama options applied per request. num_ctx is the run.py NUM_CTX constant.

protocol._comment: Verified identical across wave1/wave2 from the data (temp 0.7, seeds 1-5 => seed_base 1, reps 5, think=false). run.py --preflight now asserts the launch args + the scenarios.json content hash match these, so a stray --temp/--think or an edited prompt aborts instead of silently producing non-wave1 data.

expected._comment: Pin the Ollama version once you capture it from a known-good run (run.py stamps env.ollama_version into every row). null = record-but-do-not-enforce until pinned. wave1 did not record it, so it is currently unknown.

models_pinned._comment: If true, the preflight requires EVERY model in --models to be present locally before the sweep starts (no mid-run pull_failed like wave2's 133). Pre-pull with: while read m; do [ -n "$m" ] && ollama pull "$m"; done < data/models.txt
